key_findings: Most comprehensive fatality review: 33 ibogaine-associated deaths (1990–2018); mean age 39.5 years; 78% opioid detoxification; cardiac disease in ≥6 cases; co-intoxicants in ≥12; 1:427 death-to-treatment ratio.
contraindications: Pre-existing cardiac disease (MI, cardiomyopathy, bradyarrhythmia); QTc prolongation; Liver disease (cirrhosis, fibrosis); Schizophrenia or psychosis; Recent or concurrent opiate use; Recent or concurrent stimulant use; Concurrent hallucinogen use (e.g., ayahuasca); CYP2D6 poor metaboliser phenotype; Pulmonary embolism risk factors (immobility, IV drug use history); Alcohol withdrawal seizure risk
dosing_range: variable/unknown — retrospective review of 33 fatalities; therapeutic range 500–1000 mg (10–25 mg/kg); post-mortem blood levels 0.24–9.3 mg/L
aliases: Corkery 2018; Ibogaine fatality review 33 deaths; Progress in Brain Research ibogaine chapter

title: A Mixed-Method Analysis of Persisting Effects Associated with Positive Outcomes Following Ibogaine Detoxification
authors: Davis, Alan K.; Renn, Elise; Windham-Herman, Austin-Marley; Polanco, Martin; Barsuglia, Joseph P.
key_findings: 81% treatment responders (n=73 opioid users). Large persisting gains in gratitude, meaning, inner peace, sacredness (d=0.89–1.27). Integration was top challenge (23%).
aliases: Davis 2018; Persisting Effects Ibogaine Detox; Davis Mixed Methods Ibogaine

title: Ibogaine Acute Administration in Rats Promotes Wakefulness, Long-Lasting REM Sleep Suppression, and a Distinctive Motor Profile
authors: González, Joaquín; Prieto, José P.; Rodríguez, Paola; Cavelli, Matías; Benedetto, Luciana; Mondino, Alejandra; Pazos, Mariana; Seoane, Gustavo; Carrera, Ignacio; Scorza, Cecilia; Torterolo, Pablo
key_findings: SLEEP ARCHITECTURE: Ibogaine promotes wakefulness, robust long-lasting REM suppression, dose-dependent motor profile (tremor, flat posture at high dose). No head-shake (5HT2A marker). Uruguay polysomnography.

title: Hallucinogen Persisting Perception Disorder After Ibogaine Treatment for Opioid Dependence
authors: Knuijver, Thomas; Belgers, Maarten; Markus, Wiebren; Verkes, Robbert-Jan; van Oosteren, Toon; Schellekens, Arnt
key_findings: First reported HPPD after ibogaine (10 mg/kg HCl). Onset 48h post-dose, persisted ~5 weeks. One EMDR session produced substantial relief. QTc 420→516 ms. Patient relapsed to heroin.
aliases: Knuijver 2018; HPPD ibogaine case report; Ibogaine hallucinogen persisting perception disorder

title: Psychedelics Promote Structural and Functional Neural Plasticity
authors: Ly, Calvin; Greb, Alexandra C.; Cameron, Lindsay P.; Wong, Jonathan M.; Barragan, Eden V.; Wilson, Paige C.; Burbach, Kyle F.; Soltanzadeh Zarandi, Sina; Sood, Alexander; Paddy, Michael R.; Duim, Whitney C.; Dennis, Megan Y.; McAllister, A. Kimberley; Ori-McKenney, Kassandra M.; Gray, John A.; Olson, David E.
key_findings: LSD, DMT, psilocin, noribogaine promote neuritogenesis, spinogenesis, synaptogenesis via TrkB/mTOR/5-HT₂A. LSD EC₅₀=0.41nM. Ibogaine inactive; noribogaine active. Effects comparable to ketamine.
aliases: Ly 2018; Psychoplastogen Paper; Psychedelics Neural Plasticity

title: Changes in Withdrawal and Craving Scores in Participants Undergoing Opioid Detoxification Utilizing Ibogaine
authors: Malcolm, Benjamin J.; Polanco, Martin; Barsuglia, Joseph P.
key_findings: Retrospective chart review (N=50): COWS scores ↓ from 8.2 to 3.3, SOWS 20.5 to 10.0, BSCS 6.6 to 1.9 at 48h post-ibogaine (all p<0.01); 78% no clinical withdrawal, 79% minimal cravings; η²=0.54–0.69.
contraindications: Prolonged QTc interval; History of heart disease; Severe psychiatric conditions (psychotic spectrum, bipolar I, eating disorders); Pulmonary embolism or deep vein thrombosis; Severe respiratory conditions (emphysema, COPD); Obesity; Gastrointestinal disorders (Crohn's, IBS); Chronic infectious diseases; Cerebellar dysfunction; Organic brain disease or severe TBI; Epilepsy; Current pregnancy; Abnormal electrolytes; Impaired hepatic or renal function; Alcohol, amphetamines, cocaine, or psychiatric medications within 1 week; Buprenorphine or methadone within 4 weeks

title: Ibogaine Detoxification Transitions Opioid and Cocaine Abusers Between Dependence and Abstinence: Clinical Observations and Treatment Outcomes
authors: Mash, Deborah C.; Duque, Linda; Page, Bryan; Allen-Ferdinand, Kathleen
key_findings: MAJOR COHORT: n=191 opioid/cocaine dependent patients (St. Kitts clinic). Ibogaine (10-25mg/kg) effectively blocked withdrawal symptoms, reduced drug craving, improved mood. Safe in dose range studied with medical monitoring.
contraindications: History of stroke; Epilepsy; Axis I psychotic disorders; Cardiovascular pathology; Liver pathology; HIV/AIDS
aliases: Mash 2018; Mash; Mash Healing Visions 2018; Mash2018_Ibogaine_Detox_Transitions

title: The Effects of Ibogaine on Uterine Smooth Muscle Contractions: Relation to the Activity of Antioxidant Enzymes
authors: Oreščanin-Dušić, Zorana; Tatalović, Nikola; Vidonja-Uzelac, Teodora; Nestorov, Jelena; Nikolić-Kokić, Aleksandra; Mijušković, Ana; Spasić, Mihajlo; Paškulin, Roman; Bresjanac, Mara; Blagojević, Duško
key_findings: Low ibogaine concentrations stimulate uterine contractions; higher doses inhibit. Concentration-dependent effects linked to ATP depletion and altered redox homeostasis. SOD1/GSH-Px/CAT activity changes.
dosing_range: 1–60 mg/l ibogaine (isolated uterine tissue, cumulative dosing)

title: Understanding the Healing Potential of Ibogaine through a Comparative and Interpretive Phenomenology of the Visionary Experience
authors: Rodger, James
key_findings: Comparative phenomenological analysis through psychoanalytic vs constructivist lenses. Healing via culturally-shaped imaginal journey to unspoilt state. Argues secular framework vital for medical legitimacy.
aliases: Rodger 2018; Rodger Phenomenology 2018; Ibogaine visionary experience comparative phenomenology

title: Cardiac arrest after ibogaine intoxication
authors: Steinberg, Christian; Deyell, Marc W.
key_findings: Extreme overdose survival case: 65-70mg/kg ibogaine (5.6g total) - highest survived dose documented. Presented with ventricular flutter (270 bpm), massive QTc prolongation requiring defibrillation. QT normalised after 7 days.
contraindications: hypokalemia; unsupervised administration; non-standardised preparations (15-50% alkaloid variability); absence of prolonged cardiac monitoring; QT-prolonging co-medications; pre-existing cardiac conditions

title: DARK Classics in Chemical Neuroscience: Ibogaine
authors: Wasko, Michael J.; Witt-Enderby, Paula A.; Surratt, Christopher K.
tags: method/review; topic/mechanism; topic/18-mc; topic/receptor; topic/noribogaine; topic/pharmacokinetics; topic/cardiac; topic/history; topic/analogues; mechanism/nicotinic-receptor
key_findings: ACS DARK Classics review covering ibogaine chemistry, pharmacology, and 18-MC development. α3β4 nAChR proposed as primary anti-addiction target. Comprehensive receptor binding table across 20+ targets.

title: An Analytical Study of Iboga Alkaloids in Products from Treatment Providers
authors: Bouso, José Carlos; Oña, Genís; dos Santos, Rafael G.; Torrens, Marta
key_findings: CRITICAL: n=16 products from 5 vendors - purity ranged 0-73.4% ibogaine. One sample contained ZERO ibogaine. Extreme variability makes dose calculation impossible without analysis.
contraindications: Unverified ibogaine product purity (0-73% range found across vendors)

title: Ibogaine and Subjective Experience: Transformative States and Psychopharmacotherapy in the Treatment of Opioid Use Disorder
authors: Brown, Thomas K.; Noller, Geoff E.; Denenberg, Julie O.
tags: topic/phenomenology; topic/opioid; topic/efficacy; topic/psychiatric; method/qualitative; method/observational
key_findings: 43% of 42 ibogaine-treated OUD participants achieved complete mystical experience on SCQ; qualitative themes of confrontation, remorse, letting go, and transformation emerged
contraindications: Substance use disorders other than opioid dependence; Previous ibogaine treatment; Physical or psychosocial problems preventing compliance with study requirements
dosing_range: 22.5 ± 10.1 mg/kg HCl (Mexico, n=26); 31.4 ± 7.6 mg/kg HCl (NZ, n=14); 9.9 ± 7.8 mg/kg HCl + 18.7 ± 17.3 mg/kg crude rootbark extract (Mexico, n=4)
aliases: Brown 2019; Brown Noller Denenberg 2019; Ibogaine subjective experience OUD

title: Serotonin transporter–ibogaine complexes illuminate mechanisms of inhibition and transport
authors: Coleman, Jonathan A.; Yang, Dongxue; Zhao, Zhiyu; Wen, Po-Chao; Yoshioka, Craig; Tajkhorshid, Emad; Gouaux, Eric
key_findings: NATURE LANDMARK: First cryo-EM SERT-ibogaine structures showing outward-open, occluded, inward-open conformations. Defines transport mechanism and ibogaine non-competitive inhibition at molecular level.

title: Ibogaine Consumption with Seizure-Like Episodes, QTc-Prolongation, and Captured Cardiac Dysrhythmias
authors: Grogan, James; Gerona, Roy; Snow, Jerry W.; Kao, Louise
key_findings: Case of QTc 788ms and torsade de pointes after 2g internet-purchased ibogaine; terminated by IV magnesium; highlights unmonitored self-treatment risks
contraindications: Pre-existing cardiac disease; Concurrent opioid or cocaine use; Unsupervised self-administration; Unknown product purity or dose

title: The Iboga Enigma: The Chemistry and Neuropharmacology of Iboga Alkaloids and Related Analogs
authors: Iyer, Rishab N.; Favela, David; Zhang, Guoliang; Olson, David E.
key_findings: COMPREHENSIVE CHEMISTRY: 120+ species, biosynthesis pathways (CorS, CS, TS enzymes), total synthesis strategies, SAR for analogue development. UC Davis Olson lab review establishing synthetic foundations.

title: Ibogaine Administration Modifies GDNF and BDNF Expression in Brain Regions Involved in Mesocorticolimbic and Nigral Dopaminergic Circuits
authors: Marton, Soledad; González, Bruno; Rodríguez-Bottero, Sebastián; Miquel, Ernesto; Martínez-Palma, Laura; Pazos, Mariana; Prieto, José Pedro; Rodríguez, Paola; Sames, Dalibor; Seoane, Gustavo; Scorza, Cecilia; Cassina, Patricia; Carrera, Ignacio
key_findings: GDNF/BDNF MECHANISM: Ibogaine 40mg/kg upregulates GDNF in VTA (24h), BDNF in NAcc/SN/PFC. Dose-dependent, region-specific neurotrophin expression - mechanistic basis for lasting anti-addictive effects.

title: Ibogaine therapy for addiction: Consumer views from online fora
authors: Barber, Michaela; Gardner, John; Savic, Michael; Carter, Adrian
key_findings: Online fora serve as DIY research hubs for ibogaine seekers; users value both pharmacological 'brain reset' and visionary psychological insights; clinic preferred for safety but cost drives home use.

title: Tropane-Based Ibogaine Analog Rescues Folding-Deficient Serotonin and Dopamine Transporters
authors: Bhat, Shreyas; Guthrie, Daryl A.; Kasture, Ameya; El-Kasaby, Ali; Cao, Jianjing; Bonifazi, Alessandro; Ku, Therese; Giancola, JoLynn B.; Hummel, Thomas; Freissmuth, Michael; Newman, Amy Hauck
key_findings: Tropane-based ibogaine analogue (9b) rescued 6/13 disease-associated DAT mutants causing infantile Parkinsonism/dystonia. Novel pharmacochaperone lead for DTDS with improved therapeutic index.

title: Ibogaine and Noribogaine in the Treatment of Drug Dependency: A State of the Art Review about Safety and Efficacy
authors: Canessa, Carlos
key_findings: MSc dissertation systematic review (2000-2020): ibogaine/noribogaine safety and efficacy in substance use disorders. Confirms anti-addictive properties, cardiac risks, need for medical supervision.

title: Psychedelic Treatment for Trauma-Related Psychological and Cognitive Impairment Among US Special Operations Forces Veterans
authors: Davis, Alan K.; Averill, Lynnette A.; Sepeda, Nathan D.; Barsuglia, Joseph P.; Amoroso, Timothy
key_findings: Retrospective n=51 SOF Veterans (2017-2019): Ibogaine+5-MeO-DMT showed very large reductions in PTSD (PCL-5, d=3.6), depression (PHQ-2, d=3.7), anxiety (GAD-2, d=3.1), suicidal ideation (d=1.9), cognitive impairment (d=2.8).
contraindications: Current eating disorders; Current or past psychotic spectrum disorders; Bipolar I disorder; Symptoms of impaired reality testing; Contraindicated medications without proper taper; Cardiac abnormalities on ECG screening

title: Study of the Acute Toxicity of the Aqueous Extract of Tabernanthe iboga (Baill.) (Apocynacea) on Wistar Rats
authors: Mayindza Ekaghba, E. L.; Boukandou Mounanga, M. M.; Mathouet, H.; Mendene, H. F.; Mba Ndong, J. G.
key_findings: Aqueous T. iboga root bark extract LD50=1442.6 mg/kg oral in Wistar rats, classifying whole extract as low toxicity (Hodge-Sterner). First LD50 study of total Gabonese iboga extract.
dosing_range: LD50=1442.6 mg/kg; LD0=784.71 mg/kg; LD100=2000 mg/kg (aqueous root bark extract, oral, Wistar rats)
journal: Asian Journal of Science and Technology
aliases: Ekaghba 2020; Iboga Aqueous Extract LD50 Gabon

title: The Ibogaine Experience: A Qualitative Study on the Acute Subjective Effects of Ibogaine
authors: Kohek, Maja; Ohren, Maurice; Hornby, Paul; Alcázar-Córcoles, Miguel Ángel; Bouso, José Carlos
key_findings: Grounded-theory study (n=20) identified 8 categories and 10 subcategories of acute ibogaine effects; observer quality and self-psychoanalysis enhancement distinguish ibogaine from classical serotonergic psychedelics.
dosing_range: 6–28 mg/kg oral (i-HCl, TA, root bark, or combinations; see dosing table)
aliases: Kohek 2020; Qualitative Ibogaine Subjective Effects Taxonomy

title: A Single Administration of the Atypical Psychedelic Ibogaine or its Metabolite Noribogaine Induces an Antidepressant-like Effect in Rats
authors: Rodríguez, Paola; Urbanavicius, Jessika; Prieto, José Pedro; Fabius, Sara; Reyes, Ana Laura; Havel, Vaclav; Sames, Dalibor; Scorza, Cecilia; Carrera, Ignacio
key_findings: Single ibogaine or noribogaine dose produces antidepressant-like effect in rat FST; both compounds required simultaneously in brain, suggesting polypharmacological mechanism beyond SERT inhibition
dosing_range: 20 and 40 mg/kg IP (ibogaine HCl and noribogaine HCl); 1 and 5 mg/kg IV (ibogaine HCl)
aliases: Rodriguez 2020; Ibogaine Noribogaine Antidepressant Rats FST

title: Novel treatment of opioid use disorder using ibogaine and iboga in two adults
authors: Wilson, Claire; Millar, Trevor; Matieschyn, Zak
key_findings: Two Canadian OUD cases: Case 1 achieved total opioid abstinence (3yr+) after single treatment. Case 2 used multiple iboga sessions to taper off OAT, maintained 2yr+ abstinence. Demonstrates treatment versatility.
dosing_range: variable - flood dose protocol over 6 days
aliases: Wilson 2020; Wilson; Novel treatment of opioid

title: Death due to consumption of ibogaine: case report
authors: Aćimović, Tijana; Atanasijević, Tatjana; Denić, Kristina; Lukić, Vera; Popović, Vesna; Bogdanović, Milenko
key_findings: 27yo heroin addict died 5-12h post oral iboga powder (online-purchased); blood ibogaine 3.26 mg/L; morphine/codeine co-detected; no cardiac pathology on histology
contraindications: Active opioid use (heroin); Unsupervised/unregulated treatment setting; Online-purchased unverified product
dosing_range: unknown (iboga root bark powder, blood ibogaine 3.26 mg/L)
aliases: Aćimović 2021; Ibogaine death Serbia case report

title: The Holy Spirit of Iboga and a Contemporary Perspective on Africa's Spiritual Renaissance: Focus on Gabonese Bwiti Tradition
authors: Bekale, Audrey Nguema; Alagidede, Imhotep Paul
key_findings: Comprehensive ethnographic analysis of Gabonese Bwiti tradition tracing origins from Babongo Pygmies through modern syncretic forms; positions Bwiti as potential catalyst for African spiritual renaissance

title: A Non-Hallucinogenic Psychedelic Analog with Therapeutic Potential
authors: Cameron, Lindsay P.; Tombari, Robert J.; Lu, Ju; Pell, Alexander J.; Hurley, Zefan Q.; Ehinger, Yann; Vargas, Maxemiliano V.; McCarroll, Matthew N.; Taylor, Jack C.; Myers-Turnbull, Douglas; Liu, Taohui; Yaghoobi, Bianca; Laskowski, Lauren J.; Anderson, Emilie I.; Zhang, Guoliang; Viswanathan, Jayashri; Brown, Brandon M.; Tjia, Michelle; Dunlap, Lee E.; Rabow, Zachary T.; Fiehn, Oliver; Wulff, Heike; McCorvy, John D.; Lein, Pamela J.; Kokel, David; Ron, Dorit; Peters, Jamie; Zuo, Yi; Olson, David E.
key_findings: TBG: non-hallucinogenic ibogaine analog, ~100× lower hERG inhibition (IC₅₀ ~100 μM vs 1 μM), promotes neural plasticity via 5-HT2A, reduces alcohol/heroin-seeking in rodents with effects lasting 12–14 days
aliases: Cameron 2021; Tabernanthalog TBG; Non-Hallucinogenic Ibogaine Analog

title: Novel Class of Psychedelic Iboga Alkaloids Disrupts Opioid Addiction States
authors: Havel, Václav; Kruegel, Andrew C.; Bechand, Benjamin; McIntosh, Scot; Stallings, Leia; Hodges, Alana; Wulf, Madalee G.; Nelson, Melissa; Hunkele, Amanda; Ansonoff, Mike; Pintar, John E.; Hwu, Christopher; Abi-Gerges, Najah; Zaidi, Saheem A.; Katritch, Vsevolod; Yang, Mu; Javitch, Jonathan A.; Majumdar, Susruta; Hemby, Scott E.; Sames, Dalibor
key_findings: Columbia/Sames lab oxa-iboga alkaloids: synthetic variants maintain anti-addictive efficacy while reducing cardiac liability. Novel class development for safer analogues.

title: Safety of ibogaine administration in detoxification of opioid-dependent individuals: a descriptive open-label observational study
authors: Knuijver, Thomas; Schellekens, Arnt; Belgers, Maarten; Donders, Rogier; van Oosteren, Toon; Kramers, Kees; Verkes, Robbert
tags: topic/opioid; topic/withdrawal; topic/adverse-event; topic/protocol; mechanism/herg-blockade; method/clinical-trial
key_findings: Dutch hospital study (n=14). QTc prolongation avg 95ms (range 29-146ms); 50% reached >500ms. Prolongation >450ms lasted >24h in 6 patients. No torsade. Severe but reversible ataxia in all. 11/14 did not return to morphine.
contraindications: cardiac disease history; ventricular fibrillation history; long QT syndrome; syncope history; QTc >450ms men / >470ms women; serum potassium >5.0 or <3.5 mmol/L; severe liver dysfunction; severe renal dysfunction (MDRD <30); pregnancy; QT-prolonging medications; CYP2D6 inhibitors; psychosis history; severe depression; suicidality

title: Ibogaine: To Have or Have Not. A review of the historical, legal and ethical implications of returning control of ibogaine to drug users
authors: Lotsof, Howard S.
key_findings: Posthumous Lotsof essay on user control & drug policy: DEA officer's 50% demand reduction estimate; pharma/NIDA opposition mechanisms; ibogaine underground railroad emergence; harm reduction vs demand reduction debate.
aliases: Lotsof 2021; Lotsof To Have or Have Not

title: Serotonergic Psychedelics in Neural Plasticity
authors: Lukasiewicz, Kacper; Baker, Jacob J.; Zuo, Yi; Lu, Ju
key_findings: Noribogaine, not ibogaine, increases dendritic arbor complexity via 5-HT2A. Ibogaine neurotoxic at ≥50 mg/kg (Purkinje cell degeneration). TBG: non-hallucinogenic, non-cardiotoxic ibogaine analogue promoting spine formation.

title: Evaluating the toxicity and therapeutic potential of ibogaine in the treatment of chronic opioid abuse
authors: Luz, Matthias; Mash, Deborah C.
tags: topic/opioid; topic/pharmacokinetics; topic/noribogaine; topic/cardiac; topic/cyp2d6; mechanism/herg-blockade; method/review
key_findings: DemeRx expert opinion review. CYP2D6 metabolism, noribogaine half-life 24-30h. Reviews benefit-risk ratio for opioid withdrawal. Discusses hERG concerns and drug development barriers.
journal: Expert Opinion on Drug Metabolism & Toxicology

title: The adverse events of ibogaine in humans: an updated systematic review of the literature (2015–2020)
authors: Ona, Genís; Mendes Rocha, Juliana; Bouso, José Carlos; Hallak, Jaime E. C.; Borràs, Tre; Colomina, Maria Teresa; dos Santos, Rafael G.
tags: topic/adverse-event; topic/cardiac; topic/toxicity; topic/opioid; topic/pharmacokinetics; topic/cyp2d6; topic/combination; method/systematic-review
key_findings: PRISMA systematic review of 18 studies (2015–2020): QTc prolongation most common AE (max 788ms), TdP in 5 cases, 1 fatality. Extends Alper 2012/Koenig 2015 series.
contraindications: Pre-existing cardiovascular disease; Hypokalemia; Hypomagnesemia; Concurrent opioid/methadone use (synergistic QT prolongation); Concurrent cocaine use; CYP2D6 poor metaboliser status (2× increased ibogaine/noribogaine exposure); Hepatic impairment; Concurrent use of CYP2D6 inhibitors (e.g., paroxetine); Use of unstandardised root bark preparations (ibogaine content 0.6–11.2%); Concurrent use of P-glycoprotein substrates
dosing_range: 725 mg ibogaine HCl to 38 g dried root bark across reviewed studies; clinical trials used 20 mg ibogaine or 60–180 mg noribogaine
aliases: Ona 2021; Oña 2021; Ona Adverse Events Review; ICEERS Adverse Events Review; Ona2021_Adverse_Events_Ibogaine_Updated_Review

title: A New Approach Methodology (NAM) for the Prediction of (Nor)Ibogaine-Induced Cardiotoxicity in Humans
authors: Shi, Miaoying; Wesseling, Sebastiaan; Bouwmeester, Hans; Rietjens, Ivonne M. C. M.
tags: topic/cardiac; topic/cyp2d6; topic/noribogaine; topic/pharmacokinetics; topic/toxicity; method/in-vitro
key_findings: PBK model predicts ibogaine QTc BMDL₁₀ of 97mg/70kg; noribogaine dominates in vivo cardiotoxicity due to high plasma levels and lower protein binding
contraindications: Doses above BMDL₁₀ of ~97 mg ibogaine (70 kg body weight) predicted to cause significant QTc prolongation; CYP2D6 poor metaboliser status (up to 43-fold higher ibogaine concentrations); Concurrent CYP2D6 inhibitor use (26-fold higher ibogaine Cmax reported); Clinical doses (420–2100 mg) are 4–21-fold above predicted BMDL₁₀ for QTc prolongation
dosing_range: BMDL₁₀: 97 mg ibogaine / 70 kg (QTc onset); clinical range discussed: 420–2100 mg (6–30 mg/kg)
aliases: Shi 2021; NAM Ibogaine Cardiotoxicity Prediction; PBK Reverse Dosimetry Ibogaine

title: A narrative review of the pharmacological, cultural and psychological literature on ibogaine
authors: Underwood, Martie S.; Bright, Stephen J.; Lancaster, B. Les
key_findings: COMPREHENSIVE: Narrative review covering pharmacological (NMDA antagonist), cultural (Bwiti worldview), psychological (holotropic consciousness theory) aspects. Questions oneirophrenic classification; proposes ibogaine experience as holotropic state.

title: Ibogaine microdosing in a patient with bipolar depression: a case report
authors: Fernandes-Nascimento, Maria Helha; Viana-Ferreira, Karine; Chaves, Bruno Daniel Rasmussen; Negrão, André Brooking; Wang, Yuan-Pang
key_findings: First published ibogaine microdosing case for bipolar depression. 8mg/day for 60 days. Sustained improvement in depression, anxiety, hopelessness through day 90. No manic switch. Brazilian case.

title: Ibogaine and Their Analogs as Therapeutics for Neurological and Psychiatric Disorders
authors: Kargbo, Robert B.
key_findings: ACS Med Chem Letters patent highlight: ibogaine analogue development for neurological/psychiatric disorders. IP landscape review.

title: A Systematic Literature Review of Clinical Trials and Therapeutic Applications of Ibogaine
authors: Köck, Patrick; Froelich, Katharina; Walter, Marc; Lang, Undine; Dürsteler, Kenneth M.
key_findings: Basel systematic review (n=705 across 24 studies): efficacy for SUD withdrawal/craving; depression/trauma benefits; 2 fatalities in reviewed studies; 18-MC & tabernanthalog as safer alternatives.

title: Addiction Pharmacology and Ibogaine
authors: Malcolm, Benjamin J.
tags: topic/mechanism; topic/receptor; topic/cardiac; topic/opioid; topic/pharmacokinetics; topic/cyp2d6; topic/toxicity; topic/harm-reduction; method/review
key_findings: Pharmacist's synthesis framing ibogaine risk as manageable with screening; noribogaine QTc dose-response (16–42 ms); 18 contraindications; CYP2D6 inhibitors double exposure; fatal range 4.5–29 mg/kg
contraindications: Schizophrenia or bipolar disorder; Pre-existing cardiac illness; Prolonged baseline QTc interval; Liver damage and/or cirrhosis; Cerebellar dysfunction; Epilepsy or seizure disorders; Pregnancy; Dementia and organic brain disease; Advanced respiratory conditions; Electrolyte abnormalities (Mg, K); Untreated thyroid disorders; Medications known to prolong QTc interval; Medications that slow heart rates; Medications that inhibit CYP2D6; Psychotropic medications; Long-acting opioids; Concurrent opioid, cocaine, or other drug use (re-sensitisation risk); Acute alcohol or benzodiazepine withdrawal (seizure risk)
dosing_range: Therapeutic: 8–31 mg/kg (observational studies); estimated safe dose 0.87 mg/kg; fatal range 4.5–29 mg/kg; noribogaine dose-ranging 60/120/180 mg
aliases: Malcolm 2022; Addiction Pharmacology and Ibogaine Presentation; Spirit Pharmacist Ibogaine Lecture

title: Drug Transporters ABCB1 (P-gp) and OATP, but not Drug-Metabolizing Enzyme CYP3A4, Affect the Pharmacokinetics of the Psychoactive Alkaloid Ibogaine and its Metabolites
authors: Martins, Margarida L. F.; Heydari, Paniz; Li, Wenlong; Martínez-Chávez, Alejandra; Venekamp, Nikkie; Lebre, Maria C.; Lucas, Luc; Beijnen, Jos H.; Schinkel, Alfred H.
key_findings: ABCB1/ABCG2 modestly restrict ibogaine oral availability (2.3× AUC) and brain penetration (1.5×). Human OATP1B1/1B3 and CYP3A4 have minimal impact — reducing drug interaction risk.
dosing_range: 10 mg/kg oral (ibogaine HCl in DMSO:saline 1:9, v/v) — mouse study
aliases: Martins 2022; ABCB1 OATP Ibogaine Pharmacokinetics; Schinkel Ibogaine Transporters

title: Underground Ibogaine Use for the Treatment of Substance Use Disorders: A Qualitative Analysis of Subjective Experiences
authors: Rodríguez-Cano, Borja J.; Kohek, Maja; Oña, Genís; Alcázar-Córcoles, Miguel Ángel; dos Santos, Rafael G.; Hallak, Jaime E. C.; Bouso, José Carlos
key_findings: Drug & Alcohol Review qualitative study: underground ibogaine use for SUD treatment; subjective experience themes; unregulated setting context.

title: What We Have Gained from Ibogaine: α3β4 Nicotinic Acetylcholine Receptor Inhibitors as Treatments for Substance Use Disorders
authors: Straub, Carolyn J.; Rusali, Lisa E.; Kremiller, Kyle M.; Riley, Andrew P.
key_findings: RETROSPECTIVE ADD: Review of ibogaine and α3β4 nicotinic acetylcholine receptor inhibitors as treatments for substance use disorders.

title: Ibogaína: un psicodélico atípico con potencial antiadictivo
authors: Gallo Alfonso, Diego; González, Joaquín; Rodríguez, Paola; Castro, Santiago; Scorza, Cecilia; Torterolo, Pablo; Carrera, Ignacio
tags: topic/mechanism; topic/opioid; topic/alcohol; topic/receptor; topic/gdnf; topic/cardiac; topic/sleep; mechanism/nmda-antagonism; mechanism/kappa-opioid; mechanism/sert-inhibition; method/review
key_findings: Spanish-language review for psychiatrists covering ibogaine pharmacology, anti-addictive mechanisms (GDNF, neuroplasticity), clinical evidence summary, and cardiac safety. Includes comprehensive clinical trials table.
aliases: González 2023; Gallo Alfonso 2023; Uruguayan ibogaine review

title: (+)-Catharanthine and (-)-18-methoxycoronaridine induce antidepressant-like activity in mice by differently recruiting serotonergic and norepinephrinergic neurotransmission
authors: Arias, Hugo R.; De Deurwaerdère, Philippe; El-Kasaby, Ali; Di Giovanni, Giuseppe; Eom, Sanung; Lee, Junho H.; Freissmuth, Michael; Chagraoui, Abdeslam
key_findings: Eur J Pharmacol: (+)-catharanthine and (-)-18-MC antidepressant-like activity in mice. Differential SERT/NET recruitment. FST/TST at 40mg/kg. Coronaridine congener mechanisms.

title: A review of the mechanisms involved in the neuroprotection and neurotoxicity of Iboga alkaloids
authors: Boukandou Mounanga, Marlaine Michele; Aboughe Angone, Sophie
tags: topic/mechanism; topic/gdnf; topic/receptor; topic/toxicity; topic/noribogaine; topic/neuroplasticity; topic/analogues; topic/dopamine; method/review
key_findings: Dual neuroprotective/neurotoxic review from Gabon (IPHAMETRA/CENAREST). Crude extract LD₅₀ = 1442.571 mg/kg oral; ibogaine LD₅₀ = 263–327 mg/kg; noribogaine LD₅₀ = 630 mg/kg. σ₂ receptor affinity correlates with neurotoxicity.
aliases: Boukandou Mounanga 2023; Boukandou 2023; Iboga Neuroprotection Neurotoxicity Review

title: The Effect of Ibogaine on Cognitive Functioning
authors: Cherian, Kirsten N.; Faerman, Afik; Brown, Randi; Anker, Lauren; Keynan, Or; Ford, TJ; Keller, Jennifer; Williams, Nolan R.
key_findings: First prospective study of ibogaine (~20mg/kg) on neuropsychological performance in SOV with TBI; significant improvements in processing speed, cognitive inhibition, and verbal memory at 1-month follow-up
journal: Journal of the International Neuropsychological Society

title: Open-label study of consecutive ibogaine and 5-MeO-DMT assisted-therapy for trauma-exposed male Special Operations Forces Veterans: prospective data from a clinical program in Mexico
authors: Davis, Alan Kooi; Xin, Yitong; Sepeda, Nathan; Averill, Lynnette A.
key_findings: SOFV prospective n=86: Ibogaine 10mg/kg + 5-MeO-DMT 50mg over 3 days. Large improvements in PTSD (η²=0.414), depression, anxiety, insomnia, post-concussive symptoms at 1-month. Effects sustained to 6-months.
contraindications: Cardiac abnormalities on ECG screening; Contraindicated medications without proper taper
journal: American Journal of Drug and Alcohol Abuse

title: Ibogaine reduced severe neuropathic pain associated with a case of brachial plexus nerve root avulsion
authors: Dickinson, Jonathan E.; Díaz Inzunza, José A.; Perez-Villa, Luis; Millar, Tara G.; Pushparaj, Abhiram P.
key_findings: FIRST DOCUMENTED: 53yo male, 20-year intractable brachial plexus nerve root avulsion pain. High-dose inpatient + low-dose outpatient ibogaine protocol achieved sustained pain reduction and functional improvement.
dosing_range: 15 mg/kg flood + 38 mg/kg saturation protocol; outpatient 3–12 mg/kg

title: Transpersonal Intersubjectivity in Ibogaine Experiences: Three cases
authors: Dickinson, Jonathan
key_findings: Qualitative phenomenological study (n=3 case reports) examining transpersonal intersubjectivity in ibogaine experiences - sense of communication with ibogaine itself or entities.

title: Ibogaine Treatment in Combat Veterans Significantly Improves Sleep, Beyond Alleviating Posttraumatic Stress Disorder Symptoms
authors: Faerman, Afik; Anker, Lauren; Cherian, Kirsten; Brown, Randi; Williams, Nolan
tags: topic/veterans; topic/ptsd; topic/tbi; topic/sleep; topic/efficacy; method/clinical-trial
key_findings: n=30 SOF Veterans with TBI. Ibogaine improved PTSD, depression, anxiety, disability, cognition. Effect sizes d=1.4-4.78. No serious adverse events.

title: Three Decades of Research on the Development of Ibogaine Treatment of Substance Use Disorders: A Scientometric Analysis
authors: Fernandes-Nascimento, Maria Helha; Brooking Negrão, André; Viana-Ferreira, Karine; Rasmussen Chaves, Bruno Daniel; Yuan-Pang, Wang
key_findings: Scientometric analysis of 1,523 ibogaine publications (1993–2022) across 70 countries; USA produced 59% of output; no human RCTs identified; keyword shift from cocaine/morphine to opioids/psychedelics in third decade.
aliases: Fernandes-Nascimento 2023; Scientometric Analysis Three Decades Ibogaine Research

title: Tripping Outside the Lines: Lessons from Observational Studies of Combination Psychedelic Use Beyond Regulated Clinical Contexts
authors: Kratina, Sarah; Mayo, Leah M.
key_findings: Analysis of combination psychedelic use (including ibogaine+5-MeO-DMT protocols) in unregulated observational contexts, extracting lessons for clinical practice.
journal: American Journal of Drug and Alcohol Abuse

title: Ibogaine – A legacy within the current renaissance of psychedelic therapy
authors: Mash, Deborah C.
key_findings: Comprehensive historical review by Deborah Mash covering 100+ years ibogaine research. Documents FDA-approved Phase 1 trial (1993, IND39,680) discontinued 1995.

title: Ibogaine/Noribogaine in the Treatment of Substance Use Disorders: A Systematic Review of the Current Literature
authors: Mosca, Alessio; Chiappini, Stefania; Miuli, Andrea; Mancusi, Gianluca; Santovito, Maria Chiara; Di Carlo, Francesco; Pettorruso, Mauro; Corkery, John M.; Canessa, Carlos; Martinotti, Giovanni; Di Giannantonio, Massimo
tags: topic/efficacy; topic/cardiac; topic/toxicity; topic/opioid; topic/adverse-event; method/systematic-review
key_findings: Systematic review of 31 studies. Meta-analysis shows efficacy for SUD but significant cardiotoxicity. Most deaths in uncontrolled settings. Headache only significant adverse effect vs placebo.

title: Psychedelics reopen the social reward learning critical period
authors: Nardou, Romain; Sawyer, Edward; Song, Young Jun; Wilkinson, Makenzie; Padovan-Hernandez, Yasmin; de Deus, Júnia Lara; Wright, Noelle; Lama, Carine; Faltin, Sehr; Goff, Loyal A.; Stein-O'Brien, Genevieve L.; Dölen, Gül
key_findings: All psychedelics reopen social reward learning critical period in mice; ibogaine open state ≥4 weeks (longest tested), proportional to 36-72h human subjective effects; convergent mechanism is ECM remodelling, not 5-HT₂A
aliases: Nardou 2023; Psychedelics Critical Period; Dölen Lab Critical Period

title: Main targets of ibogaine and noribogaine associated with its putative anti-addictive effects: A mechanistic overview
authors: Oña, Genís; Reverte, Ingrid; Rossi, Giordano N.; dos Santos, Rafael G.; Hallak, Jaime E. C.; Colomina, Maria Teresa; Bouso, José Carlos
tags: topic/mechanism; topic/receptor; topic/noribogaine; topic/dopamine; topic/serotonin; topic/gdnf; topic/neuroplasticity; topic/opioid; topic/analogues; method/review
key_findings: Updates Popik 1995 review. IBO/NOR modulate ≥8 targets (MOR, KOR, DAT, SERT, NMDA, nAChR α3β4, NTFs, ABC transporters). Advocates polypharmacology paradigm.
aliases: Ona 2023; Oña 2023; Ibogaine Noribogaine Mechanistic Overview

title: Identifying setting factors associated with improved ibogaine safety: a systematic review of clinical studies
authors: Mendes Rocha, Juliana; Reis, José A. S.; Bouso, José Carlos; Hallak, Jaime E. C.; dos Santos, Rafael G.
tags: method/systematic-review; topic/adverse-event; topic/cardiac; topic/harm-reduction; topic/opioid; topic/protocol
key_findings: Systematic review of 12 clinical studies (163 completed participants): zero serious adverse events in controlled settings; all reported fatalities occurred in uncontrolled settings without medical oversight.
contraindications: Administration in uncontrolled settings without medical supervision; Absence of cardiac monitoring equipment (ECG/telemetry); Absence of pre-treatment medical screening protocols; Pre-existing cardiovascular disease or abnormalities; Pre-existing hepatic disease (e.g., chronic hepatitis C); Axis I psychotic disorders (schizophrenia, bipolar disorder, psychotic spectrum); Active suicidality or homicidality; Dissociative disorders or high risk of developing severe mental illness; Personal or family history of bipolar or psychotic disorders; Concomitant use of psychotropic medications without adequate discontinuation; Concurrent use of other substances of abuse; Unknown-purity or uncharacterised ibogaine preparations (root bark, unverified extracts); Nutritional deficiencies (risk factor for QT prolongation); QTc prolongation >500ms (emergency magnesium protocol threshold per Knuijver)
dosing_range: Ibogaine HCl 20–800 mg or 8–12 mg/kg; noribogaine HCl 3–180 mg; one study used 1800 mg encapsulated extract (75% purity) — all oral, across 12 reviewed clinical studies
journal: European Archives of Psychiatry and Clinical Neuroscience
aliases: Rocha 2023; Rocha Setting Factors 2023; Rocha Ibogaine Safety Systematic Review

title: Guidelines for Establishing Safety in Ayahuasca and Ibogaine Administration in Clinical Settings
authors: Rocha, Juliana M.; Reis, José Augusto S.; Rossi, Giordano N.; Bouso, José Carlos; Hallak, Jaime E. C.; dos Santos, Rafael G.
key_findings: USP/LEAPS clinical protocols: 72h cardiac monitoring, electrolyte targets K+ 4.5-5.5/Mg 1.5-2.5, medication tapering schedules, 3-person minimum team, trauma-informed approach framework.
contraindications: QTc >450ms (males) or >470ms (females); K+ outside 4.5-5.5 mEq/L; Mg outside 1.5-2.5 mEq/L; Uncontrolled psychiatric conditions; Personal or family history of psychosis, bipolar disorder, or schizophrenia; Recent cardiac, renal, or hepatic pathology; Pregnancy or lactation; Concurrent psychotropic medication use (especially uncontrolled)
aliases: Rocha USP 2023; Rocha Safety Protocols 2023; USP Guidelines 2023

title: A transcriptomic analysis in mice following a single dose of ibogaine identifies new potential therapeutic targets
authors: Biosca-Brull, Judit; Oña, Genís; Alarcón-Franco, Lineth; Colomina, Maria Teresa
key_findings: First transcriptomic analysis of ibogaine effects on gene expression in mouse frontal cortex (4h post-dose, 60mg/kg oral). Upregulated: hormonal pathways (Oxt, Avp), synaptogenesis genes.

title: Noribogaine acute administration in rats promotes wakefulness and suppresses REM sleep
authors: Castro-Nin, Juan Pedro; Serantes, Diego; Rodriguez, Paola; Gonzalez, Bruno; Carrera, Ignacio; Torterolo, Pablo; González, Joaquín
key_findings: Noribogaine promotes wakefulness, reduces SWS, and profoundly suppresses REM sleep in rats (20 and 40 mg/kg IP). Effects stronger than ibogaine, implicating SERT blockade as the shared mechanism.
dosing_range: 20 and 40 mg/kg IP (noribogaine·HCl in warm degassed saline)
aliases: Castro-Nin 2024; Noribogaine wakefulness and sleep effects

title: Risk Assessment of Herbal Preparations Containing Tabernanthe iboga
authors: Chen, W.
tags: topic/adverse-event; topic/cardiac; topic/toxicity; topic/pharmacokinetics; topic/noribogaine; topic/policy; topic/cyp2d6; method/review
key_findings: RIVM advises against consumer use of T. iboga; 34 sudden deaths tabulated (from 4.5 mg/kg); QTc prolongation not dose-dependent across individuals; no safe dose derivable
contraindications: Pre-existing cardiac disease (cardiomyopathy, valvular disease, coronary artery disease); History of myocardial infarction; Active neurological disorders (cerebellar dysfunction, organic brain disease, dementia); Active psychiatric disorders (psychosis, bipolar illness, major depression); Hepatic or renal impairment; Gastrointestinal disease; Concurrent use of CYP2D6 inhibitors (e.g. paroxetine); Concurrent use of QT-prolonging medications; Concurrent methadone use (extremely long half-life 15–55 hours); Concurrent benzodiazepine use; Concurrent opioid agonist use; Concurrent cocaine or stimulant use; CYP2D6 poor metaboliser status (higher ibogaine plasma levels, altered toxicokinetics); Obesity; Hypertension; P-glycoprotein substrate medications (ibogaine inhibits P-gp); Hypokalemia or hypomagnesemia; Epilepsy or seizure history; Pregnancy
dosing_range: 1–30 mg/kg bw oral (common use); 0.3–55 mg/kg bw (clinical studies); LD₅₀ 263 mg/kg bw mice, 327 mg/kg bw rats; noribogaine LD₅₀ 630 mg/kg bw mice
aliases: Chen 2024; RIVM Iboga Risk Assessment; Dutch Risk Assessment T. iboga

title: Psychedelic Therapy: A Primer for Primary Care Clinicians—Ibogaine
authors: Cherian, Kirsten; Shinozuka, Kenneth; Tabaac, Burton J.; Arenas, Alejandro; Beutler, Bryce D.; Evans, Viviana D.; Fasano, Chelsey; Muir, Owen S.
tags: topic/protocol; topic/cardiac; topic/opioid; topic/ptsd; topic/veterans; topic/pharmacokinetics; topic/electrolytes; topic/analogues; method/review
key_findings: Clinician primer covering safety (33 deaths, cardiotoxicity, Mg protection), OUD efficacy (50-65% craving reduction), veteran PTSD outcomes (86% remission). Only 1 RCT exists.
contraindications: Pre-existing cardiac conditions (coronary artery sclerosis, hypertension, myocardial infarct, cardiac hypertrophy, dilated cardiomyopathy); Prolonged baseline QT interval, including drug-induced (antibiotics, antidepressants); hERG mutations associated with long-QT syndrome; Concurrent long-acting opioids — must switch to short-acting opioids up to two weeks in advance; Poor CYP2D6 metabolism (risk factor for impaired ibogaine clearance; methadone also inhibits CYP2D6); Motor impairment conditions (Parkinson's disease, multiple sclerosis — potential contraindication, not yet studied); Concurrent medications without proper washout; Multiple concurrent substance addictions (polysubstance use increases fatality risk); History of mania or psychosis (3 reported cases, all in unregulated settings)

title: Magnesium–ibogaine therapy in veterans with traumatic brain injuries
authors: Cherian, Kirsten N.; Faerman, Afik; Brown, Randi E.; Shamma, Ahmed; Keynan, Or; Coetzee, John P.; Phillips, Angela; Bassano, Nicholas J.; Sahlem, Gregory L.; Inzunza, Jose; Millar, Trevor; Dickinson, Jonathan; Rolle, C. E.; Keller, Jennifer; Adamson, Maheen; Williams, Nolan R.
key_findings: MISTIC (Nature Medicine), n=30 SOF Veterans/TBI. Effect sizes: WHODAS d=2.20, PTSD d=2.54, Depression d=2.80, Anxiety d=2.13. Mg+Ibo prevented all QTc prolongation. No SAEs.
contraindications: Pre-existing cardiac conditions; QT-prolonging medications; Active substance withdrawal

title: Ibogaine administration following repeated morphine administration upregulates myelination markers CNP and MBP in Sprague Dawley rats
authors: Govender, Demi; Moloko, Leila; Papathanasopoulos, Maria; Tumba, Nancy; Owen, Gavin; Calvey, Tanya
key_findings: First study showing ibogaine upregulates myelination markers (CNP and MBP) in rat internal capsule post-morphine. Novel mechanism: white matter repair may contribute to addiction treatment effects.

title: Oxa-Iboga alkaloids lack cardiac risk and disrupt opioid use in animal models
authors: Havel, Václav; Kruegel, Andrew C.; Bechand, Benjamin; McIntosh, Scot; Sames, Dalibor; Javitch, Jonathan A.; Majumdar, Susruta
key_findings: BREAKTHROUGH: Oxa-iboga alkaloids (benzofuran-containing) lack proarrhythmic effects of ibogaine/noribogaine, show superior efficacy in OUD models. Potent KOR agonists with single-dose efficacy.

title: Tabernanthalog Reduces Motivation for Heroin and Alcohol in a Polydrug Use Model
authors: Heinsbroek, Jasper A.; Giannotti, Giuseppe; Bonilla, Joel; Olson, David E.; Peters, Jamie
key_findings: Tabernanthalog (TBG, 30mg/kg) efficacy in polydrug heroin+alcohol model. TBG reduced break points for both heroin (p=0.040) and alcohol (p=0.001). Non-hallucinogenic iboga analogue shows promise.

title: Disrupting Substance Use Disorder: The Chemistry of Iboga Alkaloids
authors: Hughes, Alexander J.; Hamelink, Charles R.; Townsend, Steven D.
tags: topic/analogues; topic/18-mc; topic/mechanism; topic/opioid; topic/receptor; topic/cardiac; method/review
key_findings: Comprehensive synthesis review: 20+ total syntheses of iboga alkaloids. Drug leads 18-MC (Phase II), oxa-noribogaine (κ-opioid, no cardiac risk), TBG (non-hallucinogenic, 5-HT2A plasticity)

title: The Nonclassic Psychedelic Ibogaine Disrupts Cognitive Maps
authors: Ivan, Victorita E.; Tomàs-Cuesta, David P.; Esteves, Ingrid M.; Curic, Davor; Mohajerani, Majid; McNaughton, Bruce L.; Davidsen, Joern; Gruber, Aaron J.
key_findings: Two-photon microscopy: ibogaine (40mg/kg) destabilised cognitive map in retrosplenial cortex. Supports entropy hypothesis - psychedelics disrupt constraining representations, increasing neural signalling entropy.

title: Streamlined screening platforms lead to the discovery of pachysiphine synthase from Tabernanthe iboga
authors: Kamileen, Mohamed O.; Nakamura, Yoko; Luck, Katrin; Heinicke, Sarah; Hong, Benke; Colinas, Maite; Lichman, Benjamin R.; O'Connor, Sarah E.
key_findings: Discovery of two T. iboga CYP450 enzymes: pachysiphine synthase (TiPS) and tabersonine-16-hydroxylase (TiT16H) using N. benthamiana leaf disk screening; advances understanding of MIA biosynthesis

title: The pharmacokinetics and pharmacodynamics of ibogaine in opioid use disorder patients
authors: Knuijver, Thomas; ter Heine, Rob; Schellekens, Arnt F. A.; Heydari, Paniz; Lucas, Luc; Westra, Sjoerd; Belgers, Maarten; van Oosteren, Toon; Verkes, Robbert Jan; Kramers, Cornelis
key_findings: First human PK/PD study (n=14): CYP2D6 genotype is MAJOR determinant of ibogaine metabolism - clearance varies >10-fold across activity scores. Critical for personalised dosing.
contraindications: Clinically significant cardiac disease (including ventricular fibrillation, long QT syndrome, syncope history); Baseline QTc >450ms (men) or >470ms (women); Serum potassium <3.5 or >5.0 mmol/L; Severe hepatic or renal dysfunction (eGFR <30); Concurrent QTc-prolonging or CYP2D6-affecting medications; Major depressive or psychotic symptoms

title: Ibogain a jeho potenciální využití v léčbě drogových závislostí (Ibogaine and its potential use in the treatment of drug addiction)
authors: Kobr, Hubert
tags: topic/mechanism; topic/opioid; topic/cardiac; topic/noribogaine; topic/18-mc; topic/analogues; topic/pharmacokinetics; topic/cyp2d6; topic/gdnf; mechanism/herg-blockade; mechanism/nmda-antagonism; mechanism/kappa-opioid; mechanism/nicotinic-receptor; method/review
key_findings: Comprehensive Czech review covering ibogaine mechanisms (NMDA, opioid, nAChR), CYP2D6 metabolism with sex differences, cardiac hERG risks, and safer analogue 18-MC development.

title: Multiple Episodes of Cardiac Arrest Induced by Treatment With Ibogaine: A Case Report
authors: Mestre, David; Paula, Alexandra; Gil, Francisco P.; Vaz, José
key_findings: Portuguese case: Multiple cardiac arrests after only 200mg ibogaine (2.6mg/kg test dose). QTc 636ms, torsade de pointes, 3 defibrillations. Demonstrates even low doses can be lethal. No structural heart disease.

title: Ibogaine for the Treatment of Opioid Dependence: From Mechanisms of Action to Clinical Efficacy
authors: Oña Esteve, Genís
tags: topic/opioid; topic/mechanism; topic/efficacy; topic/pharmacokinetics; topic/cardiac; method/review; method/clinical-trial
key_findings: PhD thesis comprehensively reviewing ibogaine for opioid dependence. Covers mechanisms, pharmacology, clinical efficacy, safety. ICEERS-affiliated. Supervised by Bouso and Colomina.
aliases: Oña 2024; Oña PhD Thesis; ICEERS PhD Thesis

title: Subjective ibogaine experiences across intersecting social-ecological dimensions
authors: Ozmat, Evan E.; McDonough, Alicia K.; Ladouceur, Guy M.; Roy, Darin S.; Bozek, Dana M.; Oh, Junsung; Martin, Jessica L.
key_findings: First qualitative study of ibogaine experiences across social-ecological dimensions (individual, interpersonal, systems). Consensual Qualitative Research method reveals multi-level impacts on recovery.

title: Ibogaine Induces Cardiotoxic Necrosis in Rats—The Role of Redox Processes
authors: Vidonja Uzelac, Teodora; Tatalović, Nikola; Mijović, Milica; Miler, Marko; Grahovac, Tanja; Orešcanin Dušić, Zorana; Nikolić-Kokić, Aleksandra; Blagojević, Duško
key_findings: First demonstration that oral ibogaine causes dose-dependent myocardial necrosis in rats. Lower dose (1mg/kg): focal necrosis. Higher dose (20mg/kg): diffuse necrosis affecting 60% of myocardium. Non-inflammatory mechanism.

title: Ibogaine induces juvenile-like plasticity and modulates functional and structural regulators of plasticity in the adult mouse visual cortex
authors: Acuña, Alejo; Billeri, Federico; Totaro, Valentino; Carrera, Ignacio; Mesa, Juan M.; Pizzorusso, Tommaso; Rossi, Francesco M.
key_findings: Single ibogaine dose (40mg/kg) restores juvenile-like plasticity in adult mouse visual cortex by reducing PNNs, PV interneurons, and vGAT—structural/inhibitory brakes on plasticity.

title: Old Dog, New Tricks: Ibogaine and Its Analogs as Potential Neurotherapeutics
authors: Ali, Saghir; Tian, Xiaochen; Cunningham, Kathryn A.; Zhou, Jia
tags: topic/analogues; topic/18-mc; topic/mechanism; topic/cardiac; topic/noribogaine; mechanism/herg-blockade; method/review
key_findings: Comprehensive review of ibogaine pharmacology and synthetic analogues. 18-MC and tabernanthalog show therapeutic potential without cardiotoxicity (hERG IC₅₀ >50μM and 148μM respectively). Texas allocated $50M for ibogaine clinical research.
aliases: Ali 2025; JMC 2025 Editorial; Ibogaine Analogues Review

title: Mystical experiences during magnesium-Ibogaine are associated with improvements in PTSD symptoms in veterans
authors: Brown, Randi; Lissemore, Jennifer; Shinozuka, Kenneth; Coetzee, John; Faerman, Afik; Olash, Clayton; Geoly, Andrew; Buchanan, Derrick; Cherian, Kirsten; Chaiken, Anna; Shamma, Ahmed; Sridhar, Malvika; Hunegnaw, Saron; Johnson, Noriah; Rolle, Camarin; Adamson, Maheen; Williams, Nolan
key_findings: n=30 veterans with TBI; higher MEQ30 predicted larger PTSD reductions at immediate (B=-5.89, p<.001) and 1-month (B=-4.45, p=.007) follow-up. Mystical experience also predicted decreased peak alpha frequency at 1 month. MISTIC protocol.
contraindications: History of psychotic symptoms; Neurological disorder (excluding TBI sequelae); Clinical abnormalities affecting participant safety or study integrity; Risk for suicidal behaviour during study period; Concurrent participation in another clinical trial; Contraindication to MRI
dosing_range: Test dose 2–3 mg/kg, total ≤14 mg/kg oral (ibogaine HCl, 98% pure); 1g IV MgSO₄ pre-dosing
aliases: Brown 2025; Mystical Experience PTSD Study; MEQ30 MISTIC Study

title: Ibogaine microdosing in a patient with bipolar depression: case report
authors: Carlucci, Roberta; Senhorinho, Beatriz Di Magno Camurugi; Teixeira, Isabela Neri
key_findings: Microdosing (3mg/72h, 12 weeks) in treatment-resistant bipolar II: 60% HAM-D improvement, no hypomania, stable cardiovascular. Single case, Brazilian regulatory context.

title: Case report: Significant lesion reduction and neural structural changes following ibogaine treatments for multiple sclerosis
authors: Chen, David Qixiang; Inzunza Domínguez, José Adalberto; Valle Uzeta, Juan Manuel; Pushparaj, Abhiram P.; Dickinson, Jonathan E.
key_findings: Two MS patients showed lesion shrinkage and decreased ADC values on MRI after ibogaine, suggesting remyelination. Cortical/subcortical changes in pain/emotion regions. First neuroimaging evidence for ibogaine in MS.

title: A case series of ibogaine toxicity reported to the United Kingdom National Poisons Information Service (NPIS) over a 10-year period
authors: Edwards, Ella P.; Gray, Laurence A.; Elamin, Muhammad E. M. O.; Veiraiah, Aravindan; Thanacoody, Ruben H. K.; Coulson, James M.
key_findings: 7 UK poison centre cases (2012-2022): 6/7 developed cardiotoxicity; 2 cardiac arrests with hypoxic brain injury; QTc 680-720 msec; no dose-response relationship observed; doses 5-34g root bark.
contraindications: Pre-existing cardiac conditions; Concurrent opioid use (methadone, buprenorphine); Co-ingestants (benzodiazepines, zopiclone, alimemazine); Unsupervised or unscreened settings; CYP2D6 poor metaboliser status (flagged as variable susceptibility factor, not assessed in any case)
dosing_range: Acute: 5–34 g root bark (where reported); chronic: 0.5–0.7 g root bark 5/7 days for 2 months (Patient 6); mixed preparations (root bark, ibogaine HCl tablets, capsules); dose unknown in majority of cases
aliases: Edwards 2025; UK NPIS Ibogaine Toxicity Case Series

title: Dimethyltryptamine (DMT) and ibogaine elicit membrane effects in HEK cells transiently transfected with the human 5-HT2A receptor
authors: Eliasen, Jannik Nicklas; Kristiansen, Uffe; Kohlmeier, Kristi A.
key_findings: Ibogaine (30-100μM) activates membrane currents in 5-HT2AR-transfected HEK cells via NSCC. I197V SNP shows shorter DMT response duration. First demonstration of 5-HT2AR-mediated ionic currents in this model.

title: The psychoactive compound ibogaine sex-dependently alters the firing rate and afterhyperpolarization of Ih-negative neurons in the mouse ventral tegmental area
authors: Eliasen, Jannik Nicklas; Rezagholizadeh, Amir; Jacobsen, Helene Påbøl; Kristiansen, Uffe; Kohlmeier, Kristi A.
key_findings: Sex-dependent effects: ibogaine (100μM) decreased AP firing in male VTA GABAergic neurons but not females; altered female AHP kinetics; no membrane current changes; intracellular Ca²⁺ rises in both sexes

title: A Qualitative Inquiry into Practitioners' Experiences of Using Ibogaine and Other Psychoplastogens in the Treatment of Substance Misuse
authors: Enginsoy, Ahmet
key_findings: Qualitative study of ibogaine practitioners. Identifies varied dosing strategies, importance of pre/post-treatment integration, holistic patient-centric approaches. Advocates biopsychosocial framework and adaptive regulatory guidelines.

title: Ibogaine for the treatment of Parkinson's disease: A case report
authors: Erny, Tobias; Cano Montenegro, Elba Yatziri; Barth, Joern; Noller, Geoff
tags: topic/parkinsons; topic/gdnf; topic/dopamine; topic/efficacy; mechanism/dopamine-modulation; method/case-report
key_findings: First validated-instrument case study of ibogaine for Parkinson's; 80-day low-dose treatment (25-75mg/day) showed improvements in UPDRS, PDQ-39, fatigue, depression; patient reported reduced freezing episodes and enhanced mobility
aliases: Erny 2025; GITA Parkinson's Case Report; Ibogaine PD Study

title: The Ibogaine Experience Scale (IES): Development and psychometric properties of a multidimensional measure of ibogaine's subjective effects
authors: González Espejito, Francisco; Esteban Rodríguez, Laura; Pedrero Pérez, Eduardo J.; Dickinson, Jonathan; Kohek, Maja; dos Santos, Rafael Guimaraes; Hallak, Jaime; Alcázar-Córcoles, Miguel Ángel; Morgan, Breanna Lee; Bouso, José Carlos
key_findings: PLoS ONE: 70-item Ibogaine Experience Scale (IES) development. 8 dimensions capturing oneiric/dream-like phenomenology. First validated ibogaine-specific psychometric instrument.
aliases: Espejito 2025; Espejito; The Ibogaine Experience Scale

title: Is ibogaine treatment durable? 12-month follow-up of magnesium-ibogaine therapy (MISTIC) in Special Operations Veterans with traumatic brain injuries
authors: Faerman, Afik; Lissemore, Jennifer I.; Geoly, Andrew D.; Cherian, Kirsten N.; Sridhar, Malvika; Kim, Bora; Coetzee, John P.; Anker, Lauren; Shamma, Ahmed; Keynan, Jackob N.; Brown, Randi E.; Phillips, Angela; Jester, Ashley; Bassano, Nicholas J.; Keller, Jennifer; Espil, Flint M.; Kratter, Ian H.; Rolle, Camarin E.; Williams, Nolan R.
key_findings: 12-month follow-up of MISTIC trial (n=25). Sustained large effect sizes (d>0.8) for disability, PTSD, depression, anxiety. 84% PTSD remission at 12 months. Dedicated to Nolan Williams (posthumous).

title: Ibogaine as a Tool in the Process of Overcoming Drug Addiction
authors: Gomes, Bruno Ramos; Rasmussen Chaves, Bruno
tags: topic/opioid; topic/efficacy; topic/protocol; topic/phenomenology; topic/history; topic/mechanism; method/review
key_findings: Brazilian clinical experience confirms ibogaine reduces opioid withdrawal, craving, and substance use; emphasises integration psychotherapy and proper screening as essential; estimates >30,000 treatments since 2007.
contraindications: Pre-existing cardiac conditions / widened baseline QT interval; Liver failure (elevated INR); Psychosis, schizophrenia; Bipolar disorder (risk of manic episode); Concomitant opioid use; Concomitant CYP2D6/CYP3A4 substrate medications; Recent cocaine use (QT prolongation for 7-15 days post-cessation); Low magnesium (correctable — formal but not definitive)
aliases: Gomes 2025; Ibogaine Tool Substance Addiction; Gomes Rasmussen Chaves 2025

title: Development of Bioisosteric Iboga-alkaloids as Antinociceptive and Anxiolytic Agents with Neuroprotective Effects
authors: Gupta, Abhishek; Bhattacharya, Tuhin; Pratihar, Subhamoy; Parvage, Sabnur; Sharma, Swrajit Nath; Sarkar, Arnab; De, Akash; Karmakar, Sanmoy; Dey, Sanjit; Sinha, Surajit
key_findings: Benzofuran iboga analogues (C2, C4) show antinociceptive/anxiolytic effects with improved cardiac safety. C4 showed no QTc prolongation in rat ECG. Bioisosteric replacement of indole reduces hERG blockade. BDNF/GABA/dopamine elevation.
aliases: Gupta 2025; Benzofuran Iboga Analogues; C4 Cardioprotective Analog; Indian Iboga Synthesis

title: Harnessing Pharmacogenomics in Clinical Research on Psychedelic-Assisted Therapy
authors: Halman, Andreas; Conyers, Rachel; Moore, Claire; Khatri, Dhrita; Sarris, Jerome; Perkins, Daniel
key_findings: CYP2D6 PMs show higher ibogaine exposure and lower noribogaine; halving dose recommended for PMs. CYP2D6 genotyping advised for ibogaine clinical trials.
contraindications: CYP2D6 poor metaboliser status (dose reduction to ~50% recommended for ibogaine); CYP2D6 ultrarapid metaboliser status (may require higher dose or may not be suitable candidates); Concurrent use of CYP2D6 inhibitors (e.g., paroxetine — causes phenoconversion to PM-like state); Concurrent use of CYP3A4 inhibitors (e.g., grapefruit juice — potent intestinal CYP3A4 inhibition); Liver transplant recipients (donor liver genotype controls recipient CYP2D6 phenotype); Comorbidities affecting hepatic function (cancer, liver disease — can cause phenoconversion)
dosing_range: Standard ibogaine dosing discussed in context of CYP2D6-dependent metabolism; PMs recommended ~50% dose reduction; 20 mg single dose used in referenced Glue et al. 2015 PK study
aliases: Halman 2025; Pharmacogenomics Psychedelic Therapy Review; CYP2D6 Psychedelic Review

title: Deciphering Ibogaine's Matrix Pharmacology: Multiple Transporter Modulation at Serotonin Synapses
authors: Hwu, Christopher; Havel, Václav; Westergaard, Xavier; Mendieta, Adriana M.; Serrano, Inis C.; Hwu, Jennifer; Walther, Donna; Lankri, David; Selinger, Tim Luca; He, Keer; Liu, Rose; Shern, Tyler P.; Sun, Steven; Ma, Boxuan; González, Bruno; Rodriguez, Luisina; Goodman, Hannah J.; Wu, Hunter; Sonders, Mark S.; Baumann, Michael H.; Carrera, Ignacio; Sulzer, David; Sames, Dalibor
tags: topic/serotonin; topic/dopamine; topic/noribogaine; mechanism/sert-inhibition; mechanism/dopamine-modulation; method/in-vitro
key_findings: Defines 'matrix pharmacology' - ibogaine/noribogaine modulate VMAT2 (sub-µM), SERT, OCT2 simultaneously. Dual VMAT2+SERT inhibition explains neurochemical effects. Not substrate-type releasers. No catalepsy unlike other VMAT2 inhibitors.
journal: Journal of the American Chemical Society
aliases: Hwu 2025; Matrix Pharmacology; Columbia VMAT2 Study; SynRI Paper

title: Efficient and modular synthesis of ibogaine and related alkaloids
authors: Iyer, Rishab N.; Favela, David; Domokos, Andras; Zhang, Guoliang; Avanes, Arabo A.; Carter, Samuel J.; Basargin, Andrian G.; Davis, Alexis R.; Tantillo, Dean J.; Olson, David E.
key_findings: Gram-scale 7-step ibogaine synthesis from pyridine (Olson lab). Enables analogue production. Unnatural (+)-ibogaine lacks psychoplastogenic effects. (-)-10-fluoroibogamine shows exceptional neuroplasticity and SERT modulation.

title: A Thematic Analysis of the Subjective Effects and Phenomenology of Ibogaine Administered in a Clinical Setting
authors: La Torre, Joseph; Cheung, Shirley M.; Kam, Ariana; Sackett, Nathan B.
key_findings: Largest phenomenology study (n=471). Themes: emotional amplification, life review, perceptual alteration, visionary states, ibogaine's perceived agency. Beond clinical setting. Open-ended questions capture experiential depth.

title: New Approaches to Old Targets: Re-envisioning the Synthesis of Ibogaine and Immunogenic Glycolipids
authors: Lytychevskyi, Vlad
key_findings: MSc thesis developing novel chiral and racemic synthetic routes to ibogaine via asymmetric Diels-Alder and Robinson annulation strategies; reviews all prior total syntheses

title: Ibogaine's potential role in supporting reward system recovery across diagnostic boundaries
authors: Nicolas, Mark
tags: topic/mechanism; topic/gdnf; topic/dopamine; topic/ptsd; topic/opioid; topic/psychiatric; method/review
key_findings: Transdiagnostic hypothesis: ibogaine restores reward system via GDNF, neuroplasticity, and DMN modulation. Proposes unified mechanism across OUD, depression, PTSD, TBI.

title: Treating War Trauma with Ibogaine and 5-MeO-DMT
authors: Polanco, Martin; Black, Mia
tags: topic/veterans; topic/ptsd; topic/tbi; topic/protocol; topic/combination; topic/cardiac; topic/phenomenology; method/observational
key_findings: Mission Within protocol: Ibo+5-MeO-DMT sequence for veterans, 850+ treated 2017-2023, pre-treatment EKG/electrolytes, 3-phase integration model
contraindications: Cardiac arrhythmias (pre-screening EKG required); Prolonged QT interval (specific EKG exclusion criterion); Electrolyte imbalances; Opioids, alcohol, or stimulants in participant's system (urine drug screen required on arrival); Concurrent antipsychotics; Concurrent antidepressants; Concurrent sleep aids; Concurrent antibiotics/antifungals (certain QT-prolonging types); Concurrent antiarrhythmics; Certain supplements (e.g. milk thistle — CYP2D6 inhibitor)

title: A Study of the Inhibitory and Pharmacological Chaperone Effects of Bupropion and Ibogaine on Monoamine Transporters
authors: Quinn Williams, Erin

title: Tolerabilidade e eficácia da ibogaína no tratamento do transtorno por uso de álcool: um estudo aberto e de escalonamento de doses
authors: Rocha, Jessica Maganin; Hallak, Jaime Eduardo Cecílio; dos Santos, Rafael Guimarães
tags: topic/alcohol; topic/efficacy; topic/cardiac; topic/adverse-event; topic/stimulant; method/clinical-trial
key_findings: First worldwide clinical trial of ibogaine for AUD. Dose-escalation 20–400mg HCl in N=9. Dose-dependent QTc prolongation (peaks ~496ms). 2/9 abstinent at 3 months.
contraindications: Pre-existing cardiac conditions; Long QT syndrome (baseline QTc >450ms); Concurrent QT-prolonging medications; Hepatic impairment (Child-Pugh B/C); Uncontrolled hypertension; Active psychosis or suicidal ideation

title: Studies on the iboga cults: IV. The ethnobotanical complex
authors: Samorini, Giorgio
key_findings: Defines 'ethnobotanical complex' of Bwiti: iboga as primary source with kola, cannabis, tobacco, alan, palm wine additives. Reveals multi-plant ritual system around ancestor worship.

title: Totalna Sinteza Ibogaina (Total Synthesis of Ibogaine)
authors: Šijić, Bruno

title: Risk management in a global market of alternative addiction care: a case study of the ibogaine medical subculture
authors: Söderberg, Johan; Lundgren, Jakob

title: Exploring Ibogaine/5-MeO-DMT as Treatment for the Relief of PTSD Symptoms in Veterans
authors: Staveley, Courtney D.
tags: topic/veterans; topic/ptsd; topic/tbi; topic/phenomenology; topic/psychiatric; topic/protocol; topic/combination; method/qualitative
key_findings: N=12 special ops veterans, 10-36 months post-treatment. 100% reported immediate PTSD relief. Grounded theory identifies 'gnosis' as healing mechanism—direct experiential knowledge via transpersonal encounter enables sustained transformation.
aliases: Staveley 2025; Staveley PhD; Veterans PTSD Ibogaine PhD

title: Not Losing Momentum: Cross-Sectional Insights into Ibogaine Clinical Trials
authors: Swieczkowski, Damian; Kwaśny, Aleksander; Sadko, Krzysztof; Cubała, Wiesław Jerzy
tags: topic/efficacy; topic/protocol; topic/cardiac; topic/opioid; topic/noribogaine; topic/pharmacokinetics; method/systematic-review
key_findings: Cross-sectional analysis of 9 ibogaine/noribogaine clinical trials from global registries. Documents methodological heterogeneity, early-phase dominance, and variable cardiac safety protocols. Calls for standardised framework.
contraindications: Pre-existing cardiovascular disease or prolonged QT interval (exclusion criterion across multiple trials); Major psychiatric disorders excluding target SUD (common exclusion); Recent illicit drug use (variable washout periods across trials); Concurrent methadone use without stable dosing (trial-specific); Abnormal baseline ECG findings (QTcF >450 ms in NCT06480981)
dosing_range: Ibogaine: 20–600 mg fixed or 3–12 mg/kg weight-based; Noribogaine: 3–180 mg (across 9 trials)

tags: topic/phenomenology; topic/psychiatric; topic/protocol; topic/opioid; topic/ptsd; method/qualitative; method/review

title: Magnesium–ibogaine therapy effects on cortical oscillations and neural complexity in veterans with traumatic brain injury
authors: Lissemore, Jennifer I.; Chaiken, Anna; Cherian, Kirsten N.; Buchanan, Derrick; Espil, Flint; Keynan, Jackob N.; Sridhar, Malvika; Rolle, Camarin E.; Saggar, Manish; Keller, Corey J.; Williams, Nolan R.
key_findings: EEG shows theta-alpha power increase (d=0.45–0.61), beta-gamma decrease (d=0.50–0.73), reduced neural complexity and PAF (d=1.05) post-MISTIC; PAF and LZc reductions persist at 1 month; theta/beta ratio correlates with cognitive inhibition gains.
contraindications: Concurrent psychiatric medications (required discontinuation prior to treatment); Concurrent benzodiazepine use (required discontinuation prior to treatment); Medications with potential drug–drug interactions
dosing_range: 12.1 ± 1.2 mg/kg oral (ibogaine HCl) + IV magnesium sulfate

title: Reconsidering Ibogaine for the treatment of severe mental illness and substance use disorders
authors: Yockey, R. Andrew
tags: topic/policy; topic/psychiatric; topic/opioid; topic/cardiac; topic/adverse-event; topic/analogues; method/review
key_findings: Editorial calling for ibogaine RCTs with CYP2D6 pharmacogenomics, safer analogues (18-MC, TBG), and inpatient cardiac monitoring. Reviews Texas $50M initiative and state-level policy shifts.
journal: Journal of Medicine, Surgery, and Public Health

title: Rare but relevant: Ibogaine and cardiovascular complications—prolonged QT interval and ventricular arrhythmias
authors: Brunt, Tibor Markus
tags: topic/cardiac; topic/adverse-event; topic/toxicity; topic/noribogaine; topic/cyp2d6; topic/analogues; mechanism/herg-blockade; method/review
key_findings: QTc prolongation occurs at therapeutic doses even without pre-existing cardiac conditions; CYP2D6 poor metabolisers at higher risk; safe dose ~0.87 mg/kg (10× lower than treatment doses); recommends genotyping + monitoring.
contraindications: Pre-existing cardiac conditions; QTc prolongation (>450ms males, >460ms females); CYP2D6 poor metaboliser status (genotyping recommended); Concurrent QT-prolonging medications; Doses exceeding ~0.87 mg/kg without cardiac monitoring

title: Neurorestorative Properties of Ibogaine: Linking Multi-Receptor Affinities to Remyelination and Metabolic Restoration
authors: Calvey, Tanya; Govender, Demi; Owen, Gavin; Tumba, Nancy; Lang, Dirk; Lerer, Bernard; Stein, Dan J; Shoptaw, Steven
key_findings: Proposes ibogaine's multi-receptor affinities (kappa opioid, NMDA, sigma-2) facilitate remyelination and metabolic restoration, providing mechanistic rationale for treating OUD, MS, and TBI via white matter repair
contraindications: Pre-existing cardiac conditions; CYP2D6 drug interactions; Polydrug use; Concurrent methadone or benzodiazepine use; Undiagnosed cardiovascular disease; Potassium and magnesium abnormalities
aliases: Calvey 2026; Ibogaine Remyelination Review; Neurorestorative Properties Ibogaine

title: Ibogaine: Therapeutic Potential, Cardiac Safety, and Translational Perspectives in the Treatment of Substance Use Disorders—A Scoping Review
authors: Esperança, Monica Patrícia; Gomes, Nelson G. M.; Campos, Maria Graça
key_findings: Most comprehensive cardiac safety review to date; synthesises QTc prolongation, arrhythmias, 19+ fatalities; mechanistic analysis of hERG blockade and noribogaine-driven delayed cardiotoxicity
contraindications: Pre-existing cardiac arrhythmias; Congenital long QT syndrome; Concurrent QT-prolonging medications; CYP2D6 poor metaboliser status; Hepatic impairment; Electrolyte disturbances
aliases: Esperança 2026; Cardiac Safety Scoping Review; Molecules 2026 Ibogaine Review

title: Increased cortical thickness and decreased brain age among special operations veterans with blast TBI after a magnesium-ibogaine protocol
authors: Geoly, Andrew D.; Coetzee, John P.; Buchanan, Derrick Matthew; Struckmann, Wiebke; Kim, Bora; Sridhar, Malvika; Azeez, Azeezat; Lissemore, Jennifer I.; Cherian, Kirsten; Faerman, Afik; Keynan, Jackob N.; Singal, Prakamya; Shanbour, Alaa; Bandeira, Igor D.; Kratter, Ian H.; Adamson, Maheen M.; Saggar, Manish; Rolle, Cammie; Williams, Nolan R.
key_findings: MISTIC therapy associated with increased cortical thickness in 13 regions, subcortical expansion in 8 regions, and predicted brain age reduction of 1.3 years at 1 month in SOF veterans with blast TBI (n=25)
contraindications: Pre-existing cardiac disease (QT prolongation risk with ibogaine); Drug-drug interactions (QT-prolonging medications); Pre-existing medical conditions associated with ibogaine fatalities; Overdose risk

authors: I.ACT Aotearoa/NZ

title: Clinical Guidelines for Ibogaine-Assisted Detoxification
authors: Dickinson, Jonathan; McAlpin, Jamie; Wilkins, Clare; Fitzsimmons, Christine; Guion, Paige; Paterson, Tanea; Greene, Douglas; Rasmussen Chaves, Bruno
tags: topic/protocol; topic/electrolytes; topic/cardiac; mechanism/herg-blockade; topic/opioid; topic/withdrawal; topic/cyp2d6; topic/adverse-event; topic/assessment; topic/harm-reduction; topic/noribogaine; topic/benzodiazepine; topic/alcohol
key_findings: GOLD STANDARD: Comprehensive ibogaine clinical guidelines. ISO31001 risk framework, absolute contraindications, electrolyte targets (K 4.5-5.5, Mg 1.5-2.5), medication clearance timelines.
contraindications: QTc >450ms (M) or >470ms (F); borderline 430-470ms requires cardiologist review; >500ms requires immediate hospital transfer; Schizophrenia; Bipolar disorder (hospitalised/medicated); Depersonalisation and/or derealisation disorder; Cerebellar dysfunction; Psychosis or acute confusional state; Organic brain disease or dementia; Epilepsy (rule out benzodiazepine/alcohol withdrawal seizures misdiagnosed as epilepsy); Heart failure, enlarged/hypertrophic heart; Active blood clots (PE, DVT); Major respiratory conditions (emphysema, COPD, cystic fibrosis); Severe/chronic GI issues (bleeding ulcer, leaky gut syndrome); Abnormal electrolytes — must correct to preferred ranges: K 4.5-5.5 mEq/L, Mg 1.5-2.5 mEq/L; Liver enzymes >2.5x normal; impaired kidney function; abnormal BUN/creatinine; Active infection or abscess; Within 6 months of major surgery (require physician clearance); Pregnancy; QT-prolonging medications, foods and supplements (must be cleared); CYP2D6-metabolised drugs (competition for ibogaine metabolic pathway); Serotonin-increasing medications — SSRIs, SNRIs, MAOIs, buspirone, trazodone, tramadol, St John's Wort (serotonin syndrome risk); Centrally acting drugs — blood pressure medications, barbiturates, muscle relaxants, antipsychotics, anticonvulsants, general anaesthetics (interactions not well understood); Benzodiazepine or alcohol withdrawal (seizure risk — must be stabilised, never ceased acutely; factor in multiple fatalities); Stimulant use: cocaine ≥7 days clearance; methamphetamine ≥5 days; prescription stimulants ≥5 days; caffeine ≥5 days; Antipsychotic medications — haloperidol/chlorpromazine cause prolonged negative reactions with ibogaine; must not be used as intervention; Corticosteroids (prolong QT interval; caution if required as intervention); Calcium channel blockers or beta blockers (lower BP/HR dramatically, alter cardiac conduction — beta blockers must be tapered over 7-14 days, not stopped acutely)

title: Manual for Ibogaine Therapy: Screening, Safety, Monitoring & Aftercare
authors: Lotsof, Howard S.; Wachtel, Boaz; Emery, Marc; Frenken, Geerte; Glatt, Sara; Mariano, Brian; Naeher, Karl; Polanko, Martin; Resinovic, Marko; Sandberg, Nick; Taub, Eric; Waizmann, Samuel; Wells, Hattie
tags: topic/protocol; topic/opioid; topic/withdrawal; topic/adverse-event; topic/cardiac; topic/assessment; topic/cyp2d6; topic/harm-reduction
key_findings: Foundational treatment manual establishing 15-20 mg/kg dosing, 48-hour monitoring, EKG screening, and inclusion/exclusion criteria that shaped all subsequent ibogaine protocols
contraindications: Significant cardiac artery blockage or history of myocardial infarction; Blood pressure >170/105 or <80/60; pulse >120 or <50 bpm; Uncontrolled hypertension; Concurrent drug use during treatment (potentiates opiate/stimulant toxicity); Active neurological or psychiatric disorders requiring treatment; Beck Depression Inventory score ≥24 (debated by contributing authors); Concomitant medications causing adverse interactions (anti-epileptics, antidepressants, neuroleptics); History of sensitivity or adverse reaction to ibogaine; Clinically significant abnormal laboratory values; GI, liver, or kidney disease compromising metabolism/excretion; Active peptic ulcer (nonfatal adverse events documented); Active tuberculosis; Pregnancy; Liver enzymes >400% above normal (NIDA threshold)
dosing_range: 15–20 mg/kg therapeutic; 2 mg/kg test dose; 25–300 mg/day low-dose
aliases: Lotsof Manual; Ibogaine Therapy Manual; Lotsof-Wachtel 2003; The Ibogaine Dossier; Manual for Ibogaine Therapy

title: Ibogaine: Information and Guidelines for Integrated Therapy
authors: I.ACT Aotearoa/New Zealand
key_findings: Comprehensive NZ operational manual: inclusion criteria, pre-acceptance screening, medication contraindications, emergency response protocols, consent forms, risk management for regulated therapy.
contraindications: Prolonged QT interval; Cardiac issues (pericarditis, history of cardiac problems, elevated cholesterol); Compromised liver function (active chronic HepC >4x normal range); Kidney or gastro-intestinal disease; Active neurological/psychiatric disorders (psychosis, bipolar, schizophrenia, organic brain disease, dementia requiring ongoing treatment); Cerebellar dysfunction, epilepsy; Pregnancy/breastfeeding (unless weaning plan incorporated prior to treatment); CYP2D6-metabolised drugs (inhibitors in particular; caution with inducers and substrates); Anticonvulsants, anti-epileptics, antidepressants, neuroleptics, antipsychotics; Serotonergic drugs; Centrally acting drugs; QT-prolonging drugs (caution); Substances inducing hypokalaemia or hypomagnesaemia; Steroids, hormones, depo-injection drugs (caution; corticosteroids may induce psychological disturbances); Naloxone (inhibitor of ibogaine metabolic pathway); Synthetic cannabinoids (minimum 10-day abstinence required); Alcohol (minimum 7-day abstinence prior to treatment); Methadone >50mg (must reduce to <50mg; switch to short-acting opioid preferred); Suboxone/subutex (must switch to short-acting opioid minimum 6 weeks prior)
dosing_range: 10–22 mg/kg (treatment range); 200mg test dose; minimum 72h monitoring
aliases: Patterson 2014; Patterson; I.ACT 2014; Aotearoa Guidelines 2014; NZ Guidelines 2014

title: The Ibogaine Story — Chapter 17: Cures Not Wars
authors: De Rienzo, Paul; Beal, Dana
tags: topic/history; topic/policy; topic/harm-reduction; topic/protocol; topic/mechanism; topic/phenomenology; mechanism/kappa-opioid; method/qualitative
key_findings: Documents May 1994 NIDA Phase I/II protocol design session (60-subject cohort, nested dose run-up). NAS report endorsed ACT UP demands. Molliver primate data: no toxicity 150mg/kg oral. Kappa NDE model, endogenous nor-harman discovery.
contraindications: Women excluded from initial Phase I/II trial (greater sensitivity across species); HIV+ or TB+ excluded from initial protocol; Seizures observed with intraperitoneal ibogaine injection in primates; Extreme caution in aged (rapid healing from sleep restoration could trigger latent antigens)
dosing_range: 150mg, 300mg, 600mg, 900mg, possible 1200mg (nested dose run-up); 150mg/kg oral no toxicity in primates; 50mg/kg i.p. required for ataxia in primates
aliases: De Rienzo 1995 Ch17; Ibogaine Story Chapter 17; Cures Not Wars; NIDA Protocol Design 1994

title: The Ibogaine Story — Chapter 18: Deborah Mash's Brain
authors: De Rienzo, Paul; Beal, Dana
tags: topic/history; topic/policy; topic/pharmacokinetics; topic/noribogaine; topic/opioid; topic/harm-reduction; topic/mechanism; topic/adverse-event
key_findings: Detailed insider account of 1994-95 NIDA ibogaine review, Panama treatments, Mash's noribogaine pharmacokinetics, three ibogaine-associated deaths, and political forces that blocked the Phase I/II trial.
contraindications: Pre-existing coronary artery disease (80% arterial sclerosis cited as contraindication violation in Baumann death); Concurrent opioid use at time of dosing (opiates in urine at time of Baumann treatment); CYP2D6 slow metaboliser status (prolonged noribogaine exposure implicated in Nicola K. death); Female sensitivity — lower dosing recommended (split dosing used for Nancy's third treatment)
dosing_range: 8–31 mg/kg oral (range across Panama treatments); FDA approved Miami protocol at 8 mg/kg; Baumann used ~half recommended dose; Nancy received 25, 29–31, and split 10+20 mg/kg across three treatments
aliases: De Rienzo 1995; Ibogaine Story Chapter 18; Deborah Mash's Brain; Staten Island Project

title: The Ibogaine Story — Chapter 2: Howard Lotsof
authors: De Rienzo, Paul; Beal, Dana
tags: topic/history; topic/opioid; topic/mechanism; topic/traditional-use; topic/policy; method/qualitative
key_findings: Documents Lotsof's 1962–63 ibogaine discovery: 5 of 7 addicts quit heroin/cocaine involuntarily for 6+ months; Isbell's 1955 MK-Ultra sub-therapeutic trials at Lexington; ibogaine's Schedule I placement linked to Rockefeller/methadone politics.
dosing_range: ~1 g oral for 150 lb person (threshold ~6 mg/kg); Isbell used sub-therapeutic 250 mg
aliases: De Rienzo 1997 Ch2; Ibogaine Story Chapter 2; Howard Lotsof Chapter

authors: De Rienzo, Paul; Beal, Dana
