title: The Ibogaine Story — Chapter 6: Bob Sisko
authors: De Rienzo, Paul; Beal, Dana
tags: topic/opioid; topic/stimulant; topic/phenomenology; topic/history; topic/protocol; topic/alcohol; topic/harm-reduction; method/qualitative
key_findings: Sisko's 9 paraclinical treatments (1987-1991) — first new human ibogaine trials in 25 years. Ibogaine interrupts cigarettes and methadone. Multiple case studies with Bwiti phenomenology.
contraindications: Enabling behaviour from untreated partner (methadone smuggling documented); Nausea/vomiting risk requiring anti-emetic protocol; Pre-existing cardiac conditions (Baumann fatality reference — weak heart)
dosing_range: Weight-adjusted oral ibogaine HCl (99.7% purity); booster doses administered 1.5hr post-initial if needed; rectal aqueous solution as emergency anti-emetic route
aliases: De Rienzo 1995 Ch6; Ibogaine Story Chapter 6; Bob Sisko Chapter; Sisko Nine Case Histories; ICASH Chapter

title: The Ibogaine Story — Chapter 7: Stanley Glick
authors: De Rienzo, Paul; Beal, Dana
tags: topic/mechanism; topic/dopamine; topic/opioid; topic/stimulant; topic/receptor; topic/pharmacokinetics; topic/noribogaine; mechanism/dopamine-modulation; mechanism/kappa-opioid; method/preclinical
key_findings: First dose-response data: 5mg/kg gives 40% morphine reduction, 40mg gives 90%. After-effect persisted weeks. Transient kappa-opioid binding. Long-lasting metabolite hypothesis prefigures noribogaine.
dosing_range: 2.5–80 mg/kg IP ibogaine in rats; 5–40 mg/kg IP dose-response on morphine self-administration; 40–80 mg/kg IP for persistent after-effect
aliases: De Rienzo 1995 Ch7; Ibogaine Story Chapter 7; Stanley Glick Chapter; Glick Dose-Response

title: The Ibogaine Story — Chapter 8: Nico Adriaans
authors: De Rienzo, Paul; Beal, Dana
tags: topic/phenomenology; topic/opioid; topic/history; topic/harm-reduction; topic/withdrawal; method/qualitative
key_findings: Nico Adriaans (founder, Dutch Junkiebond) treated May 1990 for 0.75g/day heroin. Three-phase phenomenology documented. Inadvertent cigarette interruption. Methadone 180 to 30mg.
dosing_range: Weight-adjusted oral ibogaine HCl (allergy test dose 10mg, then full dose in two halves 30min apart)
aliases: De Rienzo 1995 Ch8; Ibogaine Story Chapter 8; Nico Adriaans Chapter; Nico Adriaans Treatment

title: Learning from ibogaine fatalities, rather than covering them up
authors: Evans, Jules
key_findings: Documents 5 ibogaine fatalities (2024-2026): Ambio, SoulCentro, IbogaQuest, Holos/Costa Rica, Haman/Colorado; advocates transparency over cover-ups; cites 1-in-427 fatality estimate; GITA warnings on nitazene/xylazine detection failures
dosing_range: Not specified (flood dose protocols at various clinics)

title: Strategic Implementation of Adjunctive Long-Acting Benzodiazepines & Cannabidiol-Rich Cannabis Oil
authors: Kagalovsky, Philip
tags: topic/protocol; topic/combination; topic/benzodiazepine; topic/cardiac; topic/opioid; topic/harm-reduction
key_findings: FOUNDATIONAL PHARMACOLOGY: Ibogaine has negligible GABAergic effects (Popik 1995) and doesn't bind CB1/CB2 receptors (Li 2017), therefore benzodiazepines/cannabis NOT contraindicated unlike other drugs
contraindications: Very low resting HR (<50bpm); BP ≤90/60 (without BP medication); History of adverse reactions to cannabis (for CBD component); Psychosis history (for cannabis component)
dosing_range: adjunct protocol: 10–30 mg diazepam; CBD oil titrated to response

title: Kush in Sierra Leone: West Africa's Growing Synthetic Drugs Challenge
authors: Bird Ruiz Benitez de Lugo, Lucia; de Bruijne, Kars
key_findings: Kush contains nitazenes (up to 25× fentanyl potency) and MDMB-4en-PINACA. First confirmed nitazene penetration of West African markets.
aliases: Kush Report 2025; GI-TOC Kush Study; Sierra Leone Synthetics Report
publisher: Global Initiative Against Transnational Organised Crime / Clingendael Institute

title: Ibogaine: A Clinical Summary
authors: Brown, Thomas Kingsley
key_findings: Industry marketing document, contradicts Alper 2012 on deaths, safety claims vs evidence mismatch.
contraindications: Certain cardiovascular conditions (unspecified — refers to clinical guidelines); Coincident withdrawal from alcohol or benzodiazepines; Substances metabolised by CYP450-2D6 (including grapefruit and quinine)
